mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when either series declines, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5% annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, or the negative correlation vanishes when Fed policy remains accommodative.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising wage income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized GDP growth exceeding 3.0% during late-cycle expansions triggers expectations of preemptive monetary tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% in late-cycle phases, or the mechanism loses predictive power when inflation remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms synchronized supply and labor market strength, generating immediate positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.
